title: Tamil Beans Usili

ingredients.name: green beans, cut in 1 cm pieces

subs.name: raw banana (vazhakkai), cubed and boiled — gives the dish a starchier, heartier character

ingredients.name: chana dal (kadalai paruppu)
visual_checks: soak in water for 30 minutes before grinding
ingredients.notes: soak in water for 30 minutes before grinding

subs.name: toor dal, soaked and ground the same way — slightly softer in texture
cumin seeds — the flavour shifts slightly but works well

steps.text: Soak the chana dal and dried red chillies together in cold water for 30 minutes. Drain and grind in a mixie to a coarse, slightly grainy paste — do not add water; the paste should be thick and able to hold its shape. Add salt, turmeric, and fennel seeds to the paste and mix.

Shape the dal paste into a loose flat disc on a greased plate or banana leaf. Steam in a pressure cooker (without weight, lid on) or idli steamer for 10 minutes until set and cooked through. Remove and cool for 5 minutes, then crumble into pea-sized lumps by hand.
Blanch the beans in boiling salted water for 3 minutes until just tender but still bright green. Drain and set aside — do not overcook or the texture contrast is lost.
Heat oil in a kadai over medium flame. Splutter mustard seeds, add urad dal and fry until golden. Add curry leaves and asafoetida and fry for 20 seconds.
Add the crumbled dal to the kadai and stir-fry on medium heat for 4 minutes, breaking up any larger clumps, until the crumble turns golden and a little crispy in places.

Add the blanched beans to the kadai and toss to combine with the dal crumble. Cook on medium-high for 2 minutes, mixing constantly. Check salt and serve hot — the dish should be completely dry with no liquid.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: beans → raw banana (vazhakkai), cubed and boiled — gives the dish a starchier, heartier character; chana-dal → toor dal, soaked and ground the same way — slightly softer in texture; fennel → cumin seeds — the flavour shifts slightly but works well
